title: VLM Edit Plan
description: A structured edit plan produced by a video-language model. Describes editorial decisions for a single source video. The renderer executes this plan deterministically — every editorial judgment lives here, not in the renderer.

source_video.description: Metadata about the input video. Populated by the pipeline before the VLM sees it, so the model knows what it's working with.

fps.description: Frames per second of the source.

segments.description: An ordered list of time segments that partition the entire source video. Every second of the source must be covered by exactly one segment — no gaps, no overlaps. This is the backbone of the edit: it defines what stays, what gets cut, and the pacing of the final video.

action.description: 'keep' = include in the final video. 'cut' = remove entirely.

cut_reason.description: Why this segment was cut. Required when action='cut'. Helps the judge evaluate whether cuts are justified.

speed.description: Playback speed multiplier for this segment. 1.0 = normal. Only applies when action='keep'. Use sparingly on speech — listeners notice speed changes above 1.25x or below 0.8x.

transition_in.description: Transition effect applied at the START of this segment (i.e., how we enter it from the previous segment). Only meaningful for 'keep' segments that follow a cut.

duration_s.description: Duration of the transition in seconds.

captions.description: Caption configuration and word-level timing. Captions track the speaker's words. The model decides: which words to display, how to group them, and what emphasis to apply. Timing comes from Whisper and is pre-populated — the model's job is grouping and emphasis.

enabled.description: Whether captions should be rendered at all.

grouping.description: How words are chunked into visible groups. 'word_by_word' = each word appears individually (TikTok-style). 'phrase' = 2-5 words appear together. 'sentence' = full sentences. This is an editorial decision about pacing and readability.

words.description: Word-level caption data. Timestamps come from Whisper. The model adds emphasis annotations.

start_s.description: When the word starts being spoken.

end_s.description: When the word finishes being spoken.

emphasis.description: Visual emphasis for this word. The model should emphasize key terms, not random words. 'highlight' = background color behind word. 'bold' = heavier weight. 'color_pop' = different color from surrounding words.

zooms.description: Zoom events. Each zoom specifies when to punch in, how far, and where to focus. Good editors zoom on moments of emphasis — a key point, a punchline, a dramatic pause. Bad editors zoom randomly. The model's zoom placement is a strong signal of editing taste.

start_s.description: When the zoom begins (in source video time).

end_s.description: When the zoom ends and the frame returns to normal.

scale.description: Zoom multiplier. 1.0 = no zoom. 1.3 = typical 'punch in'. 2.0+ = extreme closeup. Values above 1.5 on 1080p footage will look pixelated.

anchor.description: What the zoom focuses on. 'face' = center on detected face (most common for talking-head). 'center' = geometric center of frame.

anchor_xy.description: Custom anchor point as normalized coordinates. Only used when anchor='custom'.

easing.description: How the zoom animates in. 'spring' gives a snappy, energetic feel (TikTok). 'ease_in_out' is smoother (professional).

overlays.description: Image or graphic overlays inserted into the frame. These are B-roll images, diagrams, memes, or illustrations that appear on screen while the speaker talks. The model decides WHAT to show, WHEN, and WHERE — the image source is either a URL or a search query that the pipeline resolves. Good overlay placement shows a relevant image exactly when the speaker references a concept.

start_s.description: When the overlay appears (source video time).

image_query.description: A descriptive search query for the overlay image. The pipeline uses this to fetch a relevant image. e.g., 'python logo', 'confused math meme', 'neural network diagram'. This tests whether the model understands what visual would complement the speech.

image_url.description: Direct URL to an image. Used when the image is pre-specified rather than searched. Optional — either image_query or image_url should be provided.

position.description: Where the overlay appears on screen. 'fullscreen' = replaces the video frame entirely (true B-roll). 'picture_in_picture' = small inset while speaker remains visible.

animation.description: How the overlay enters the frame.

text_overlays.description: On-screen text that is NOT caption/subtitle text. Title cards, section headers, key stats, callout labels. The model writes the text content and decides placement. This tests content comprehension — can the model identify the key takeaway of a segment and surface it visually?

text.description: The text to display. Keep it short — this is a visual label, not a paragraph.

style.description: Visual style of the text overlay. The renderer interprets these as visual presets (font size, background, animation). The model's job is choosing the right one for the context.

music.description: Background music / audio bed. The model selects mood and energy level; the pipeline maps this to a track from a pre-built library. The editorial decisions are: should there be music at all? What energy? When should it start/stop? Should it duck under speech?

mood.description: The mood/energy of the background track.

start_s.description: When the music starts (source video time).

end_s.description: When the music ends. Defaults to end of video if omitted.

volume.description: Volume level relative to speech. 0.1-0.2 is typical for background music under speech.

duck_under_speech.description: Whether to automatically lower music volume when speech is detected.

reframe.description: Global reframing / aspect ratio change. Decides whether to crop the video to a different aspect ratio (e.g., 16:9 source to 9:16 for TikTok/Reels). This is a single global decision, not per-segment.

focus.description: How to position the crop. 'face_track' = keep the face centered as it moves.
